title: phaseolus helvolus var. missouriensis
intro: Phaseolus helvolus var. missouriensis, also known as Missouri bean or wild bean, is a perennial legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Phaseolus helvolus var. missouriensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's beans and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
